Integration</span></div><h1 class="font-display text-[2rem] leading-[1.2] font-bold tracking-tight text-balance text-slate-900 dark:text-white"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#fixPrimer" id="fixPrimer">FIX Primer<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h1></header><div class="text-textSecondary mb-9 w-full border-b border-slate-200 pb-9 text-base dark:border-slate-700">A brief introduction to the FIX protocol</div><astro-slot> <p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base "><strong>Stop</strong>: If you are familiar with the FIX protocol you can most likely skip
this section. The <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="/documentation/fix/fix-42/#fixDocs">OneChronos FIX Spec</a> contains everything that you need to integrate OneChronos. If you are looking for documentation on FIX itself, please <strong>proceed.</strong></p>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">The FIX protocol is a transport agnostic text based protocol widely used by
financial institutions to send orders, receive execution reports, and
disseminate market data. There are many versions of FIX, and individual
institutions usually customize a specific version of FIX for their use case.
OneChronos FIX is based on the <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="https://fixtrading.org/packages/fix-4-2-specification-with-errata-20010501/">OneChronos FIX Spec</a>.</p>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">FIX is a session based, asynchronous, guaranteed message delivery protocol.
Sessions are bidirectional streams of messages between two parties that may
arrive out-of-order, but will always be processed by the receiving application
in-order. A single FIX session can span multiple physical connections; sessions
are independent of network connects and disconnects. Message sequence numbers
are used to ensure orderly message processing and recovery of lost messages.</p>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">FIX messages are broadly divided into two types: administrative, which control
the FIX session itself, and business, which exchange information on the
application level. The messages themselves are key/value pairs, delimited by
the <code>SOH</code> character (<code>ASCII #001, hex 0x01</code>). Given that <code>SOH</code> is
an unprintable character, we’ll use ”|” in its place when displaying messages.
All FIX messages start with the Standard Message Header and end with the
Standard Message Trailer. The set of key/value pairs between the header and the
trailer (referred to as the Body) are message type dependent. The type of the
message is always given by a tag in the header: <code>MsgType (tag #35)</code>.</p>
<h2 class="DocSearch-lvl2 mt-10 mb-3 border-b border-slate-200 pb-2.5 text-xl font-semibold tracking-tight text-slate-900 dark:border-slate-700 dark:text-white"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#message-format" id="message-format">Message Format<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h2>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">Every FIX message is a stream of <code><tag>=<value></code> fields with a field
delimiter (ASCII <code>SOH</code>) used between fields in the stream. Depending on the
message type, some fields are required, others are conditionally required, and
some are optional. All tags must have a value specified. Optional fields without
values should not be included in the FIX message - the message will be rejected
as malformed if they are. The general format of a FIX message is the <a href="/documentation/fix/fix-42/#standard-header" class="text-ocxPrimary font-normal hover:underline">standard
header</a> followed by the message body fields and terminated
with the <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="/documentation/fix/fix-42/#fix42StandardTrailer">standard trailer</a>.</p>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">Except where noted, fields within a message can be defined in any sequence. The
relative position of a field within a message is inconsequential (although by
convention they are displayed in ascending tag sorted order).</p>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">With the exception of <code>data</code> type fields discussed in <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="#fix42DataTypes">data types</a> FIX uses ASCII 128 encoding for all message content (tags, values, and delimiters).</p>
<h3 class="DocSearch-lvl3 mt-7 mb-2 text-[1.0625rem] leading-snug font-semibold text-slate-900 dark:text-white"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#field-delimiter" id="field-delimiter">Field Delimiter<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h3>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">All fields (including the last field in a message) are terminated by the ASCII
<code>SOH</code> character (<code>#001, hex 0x01</code>). Given that this is an unprintable
character, our documentation uses ”|” in place of <code>SOH</code>, but <code>SOH</code>
should always be used in actual FIX messages.</p>
<h3 class="DocSearch-lvl3 mt-7 mb-2 text-[1.0625rem] leading-snug font-semibold text-slate-900 dark:text-white"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#fix42DataTypes" id="fix42DataTypes">Data Types<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h3>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">The following data type descriptions were taken from the <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="https://fixtrading.org/packages/fix-4-2-specification-with-errata-20010501/">Financial Information Exchange Protocol (FIX) Version 4.2 with Errata 20010501</a>. Data types are mapped to ASCII strings as follows:</p>
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li><strong>int</strong>: Sequence of digits without commas or decimals and optional sign
character (ASCII characters ”-” and “0” - “9” ). The sign character utilizes
one byte (i.e. positive int is “99999” while negative int is “-99999”). Note
that int values may contain leading zeros (e.g. “00023”= “23”). Examples:
723 in field 21 would be mapped int as |21=723|. -723 in field 12 would be
mapped int as |12=-723|</li>
<li><strong>float</strong>: Sequence of digits with optional decimal point and sign character
(ASCII characters ”-”, “0” - “9” and ”.”); the absence of the decimal point
within the string will be interpreted as the float representation of an
integer value. All float fields must accommodate up to fifteen significant
digits. The number of decimal places used should be a factor of
business/market needs and mutual agreement between counterparties. Note that
float values may contain leading zeros (e.g. “00023.23” = “23.23”) and may
contain or omit trailing zeros after the decimal point (e.g. “23.0” =
“23.0000” = “23”).</li>
<li><strong>qty</strong>: float field (see definition of “float” above) capable of storing
either a whole number (no decimal places) of “shares” or a decimal value
containing decimal places for non-share quantity asset classes.</li>
<li><strong>price</strong>: float field (see definition of “float” above) representing a price.
Note the number of decimal places may vary.</li>
<li><strong>char</strong>: Single character value, can include any alphanumeric character or
punctuation except the delimiter. All char fields are case sensitive (i.e. m ≠
M).</li>
<li><strong>boolean</strong>: a char field (see definition of “char” above) containing one of
two values:
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>‘Y’ = True/Yes</li>
<li>‘N’ = False/No</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><strong>string</strong>: Alpha-numeric free format strings, can include any character or
punctuation except the delimiter. All char fields are case sensitive (i.e.
morstatt ≠̸ Morstatt).</li>
<li><strong>multi_value_string</strong>: String field (see definition of “String” above)
containing one or more space delimited values.</li>
<li><strong>exchange</strong>: String field (see definition of “String” above) representing a
market or exchange.</li>
<li><strong>utctimestamp</strong>: Time/date combination represented in UTC (Universal Time
Coordinated, also known as “GMT”) in either YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS (whole seconds)
or YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.sss (milliseconds) format, colons, dash, and period
required. Valid values:
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31, HH = 00-23, MM = 00-59,
SS = 00-5960 (60 only if UTC leap second*) (without milliseconds).</li>
<li>YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31, HH = 00-23, MM = 00-59,
SS = 00-5960 (60 only if UTC leap second*), sss=000-999
(indicating milliseconds).</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">* <em>See the <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="https://www.usno.navy.mil/USNO/time/master-clock/leap-seconds">International Earth Rotation Service (IERS) guide to leap seconds</a> for additional details on leap second handling.</em></p>
<h3 class="DocSearch-lvl3 mt-7 mb-2 text-[1.0625rem] leading-snug font-semibold text-slate-900 dark:text-white"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#checksum-calculation" id="checksum-calculation">Checksum Calculation<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h3>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">FIX uses a weak checksum to protect against obvious forms message corruption.
The <code>CheckSum (tag #10)</code> is the last field of any FIX message. It is
computed via the following procedure:</p>
<ol class="text-textPrimary list-inside list-decimal px-2 py-4 text-base font-normal">
<li>Initialize an unsigned 32 bit integer (henceforth referred to as
checksumTotal) to zero.</li>
<li>Iterate over every character in the FIX message up to, but not including, the
checksum field, and add its integer value to checksumTotal.</li>
<li>Take the value of <code>checksumTotal mod 256</code> and format it as a zero padded,
three digit string.</li>
</ol>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">In pseudocode:</p>
<div class="relative overflow-visible"><div class="absolute top-[-12px] right-3 flex text-xs text-white"><div class="rounded-l-lg rounded-r-none border bg-slate-900 p-2">c</div><button class="group inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-full py-2 px-4 text-sm font-semibold focus:outline-none focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 bg-slate-900 text-white hover:bg-slate-700 hover:text-slate-100 active:bg-slate-800 active:text-slate-300 focus-visible:outline-slate-900 rounded-l-sm rounded-r-lg border-t border-r border-b px-2 py-0" type="button" aria-label="Click to copy"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-1 w-4"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M15.75 17.25v3.375c0 .621-.504 1.125-1.125 1.125h-9.75a1.125 1.125 0 0 1-1.125-1.125V7.875c0-.621.504-1.125 1.125-1.125H6.75a9.06 9.06 0 0 1 1.5.124m7.5 10.376h3.375c.621 0 1.125-.504 1.125-1.125V11.25c0-4.46-3.243-8.161-7.5-8.876a9.06 9.06 0 0 0-1.5-.124H9.375c-.621 0-1.125.504-1.125 1.125v3.5m7.5 10.375H9.375a1.125 1.125 0 0 1-1.125-1.125v-9.25m12 6.625v-1.875a3.375 3.375 0 0 0-3.375-3.375h-1.5a1.125 1.125 0 0 1-1.125-1.125v-1.5a3.375 3.375 0 0 0-3.375-3.375H9.75"></path></svg></button></div><pre class="fence-code overflow-x-auto rounded-lg bg-slate-900 p-4 text-sm"><code class="language-c"><span class="token keyword">char</span><span class="token operator">*</span> <span class="token function">GenerateCheckSum</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token keyword">char</span><span class="token operator">*</span> buf<span class="token punctuation">,</span> <span class="token keyword">long</span> bufLen<span class="token punctuation">)</span>
<span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token keyword">static</span> <span class="token keyword">char</span> tmpBuf<span class="token punctuation">[</span><span class="token number">4</span><span class="token punctuation">]</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
    <span class="token keyword">long</span> idx<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
    <span class="token keyword">unsigned</span> <span class="token keyword">int</span> cks<span class="token punctuation">;</span>

    <span class="token keyword">for</span> <span class="token punctuation">(</span>idx <span class="token operator">=</span> <span class="token number">0L</span><span class="token punctuation">,</span> cks <span class="token operator">=</span> <span class="token number">0</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span> idx <span class="token operator"><</span> bufLen<span class="token punctuation">;</span> cks <span class="token operator">+=</span> <span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token keyword">unsigned</span> <span class="token keyword">int</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span>buf<span class="token punctuation">[</span>idx<span class="token operator">++</span><span class="token punctuation">]</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span> <span class="token punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="token punctuation">}</span>

    <span class="token function">sprintf</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>tmpBuf<span class="token punctuation">,</span> <span class="token string">"%03d"</span><span class="token punctuation">,</span> <span class="token punctuation">(</span><span class="token keyword">unsigned</span> <span class="token keyword">int</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">(</span>cks <span class="token operator">%</span> <span class="token number">256</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">)</span><span class="token punctuation">;</span>
    <span class="token keyword">return</span> tmpBuf<span class="token punctuation">;</span>
<span class="token punctuation">}</span>
</code></pre></div>
<h2 class="DocSearch-lvl2 mt-10 mb-3 border-b border-slate-200 pb-2.5 text-xl font-semibold tracking-tight text-slate-900 dark:border-slate-700 dark:text-white"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#fix42MessageDelivery" id="fix42MessageDelivery">Message Delivery<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h2>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">FIX guarantees full, ordered, once-and-only-once processing (of application
messages) between parties regardless of the network transport (e.g. TCP/IP, UDP)
used. Formally, FIX provides bidirectional at-least-once message delivery
semantics, and once-and-only-once processing semantics. A system of inbound and
outbound sequence numbers tracked by both sides of the session dialogue makes
this possible. Henceforth we will refer to the two parties in a FIX dialogue as
P1 and P2. Note that P1’s outbound sequence is P2’s inbound sequence, and P1’s
inbound sequence is P2’s outbound sequence. By tracking sequences and providing
facilities for P1 to request that P2 resends messages (and vice versa) the
protocol allows for asynchronous but orderly communication. Note that FIX uses
an optimistic delivery model. Message receipt is never acknowledged on the
protocol layer but message processing is (e.g. <code>NewOrderSingle</code> is
acknowledged with an <code>ExecutionReport</code> indicating the order’s status).</p>
<h3 class="DocSearch-lvl3 mt-7 mb-2 text-[1.0625rem] leading-snug font-semibold text-slate-900 dark:text-white"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#fix42SequenceResets" id="fix42SequenceResets">Sequence Numbers<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h3>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">Every FIX message contains a sequence number - <code>MsgSeqNum (tag #34)</code> - in
its header. Parties to a FIX session use these sequence numbers to ensure that
messages arriving from their counterparty are processed in the correct order,
and that no messages were lost.</p>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">At the beginning of a FIX session both parties initialize their “inbound” and
“outbound” sequence numbers to one. When a message is sent, they increment their
outbound counter. When a message is received, they check that the message
sequence number is equal to their inbound sequence number. If it is, they
increment their inbound sequence number and process the message. If it is not,
the process described in <a href="#ordered-message-processing" class="text-ocxPrimary font-normal hover:underline">ordered message
processing</a> is used to recover the dropped
messages. Note that in both cases the inbound/outbound sequence numbers were
read and the appropriate action performed before incrementing.</p>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">Example:</p>
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Start of session: P1 sets her inbound and outbound sequence number to one,
as does P2</li>
<li>P1 sends P2 a <code>Logon</code> message with sequence number one. After sending,
she increments her sequence number to two</li>
<li>P2 receives the <code>Logon</code> message from P1, noting that one is the expected
sequence number at the start of the session. He sends a <code>Logon</code> message
in acknowledgement. The sequence number on his response is one (he hasn’t
sent anything yet). After sending, he increments his outbound sequence number
to two</li>
<li>P1 receives the message, noting that one was the expected sequence number for
P2’s response</li>
<li>Both P1 and P2 now have their inbound and outbound sequence numbers set to
two. Going forward P1 and P2 will not always increment their sequence numbers
in lock step. Some messages never need a reply (market data) and others may
need several replies (multiple execution reports when an order is filled).
Having P1 and P2 maintain independent inbound/outbound sequence numbers
ensures that this is never a problem</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="DocSearch-lvl3 mt-7 mb-2 text-[1.0625rem] leading-snug font-semibold text-slate-900 dark:text-white"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#fix42OrderedProcessing" id="fix42OrderedProcessing">Ordered Message Processing<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h3>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">FIX supports two options for processing out-of-order/dropped messages. In both
cases, a <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="/documentation/fix/fix-42/#fix42ResendRequest">ResendRequest</a> as outlined in the section on <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="#fix42Recovery">recovery</a> is used to initiate retransmission. Of the two options below, option one is preferable, but both are valid:</p>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">When a message arrives with a sequence number higher than the expected inbound
sequence number…</p>
<ol class="text-textPrimary list-inside list-decimal px-2 py-4 text-base font-normal">
<li>Request the missing message(s) explicitly by sequence number while placing
new arrivals into to a priority queue sorted by message sequence number.
When the missing message is received as a retransmission, process it, process
the queue, and resume processing of the inbound message stream.
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Example: the receiver misses message five and receives messages six
through ten. The receiver requests that message five be replayed and adds
messages six through ten to a sorted list for processing after message
five is received.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Ignore all new messages after the gap and request replay of messages between
the inbound sequence number and the largest message sequence number seen
(or preferably set <code>EndSeqNo=0</code> in the <code>ResendRequest</code> to indicate
that all messages from <code>BeginSeqNo</code> on should be replayed). If new
messages arrive while the replayed message(s) are in-flight, repeat the
process iteratively.
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Example: the receiver processes messages one through four, misses message
five, and receives messages six through ten. It requests replay for
messages five through ten by sending a <code>ResendRequest</code> with
<code>BeginSeqNo=5</code> and <code>EndSeqNo=10</code> or ideally <code>EndSeqNo=0</code>.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>
<h3 class="DocSearch-lvl3 mt-7 mb-2 text-[1.0625rem] leading-snug font-semibold text-slate-900 dark:text-white"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#fix42Recovery" id="fix42Recovery">Recovery<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h3>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">As noted in the section on <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="#fix42OrderedProcessing">ordered message processing</a>, counterparties can request that missed messages are replayed via a <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="/documentation/fix/fix-42/#fix42ResendRequest">ResendRequest</a> message. Any message that is sent in response to a replay request must be sent with the <code>PossDupFlag (tag #43)</code> set to <code>Y</code>. The only fields that can change in a retransmission are: <code>CheckSum (tag #10)</code>, <code>OrigSendingTime (tag #122)</code>, <code>SendingTime (tag #52)</code>, <code>BodyLength (tag #9)</code> and <code>PossDupFlag (tag #43)</code>.</p>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">A counterparty may elect to send a <code>SequenceReset</code> message with the
<code>GapFillFlag (tag #123)</code> in response to <code>ResendRequest</code> in lieu of
replaying the original message. This is useful in situations where the original
message is no longer relevant, e.g. an outdated order, or when the message
requested is an administrative message (which typically aren’t replayed).</p>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">When consecutive administrative messages are present it is recommended that only
one <code>SequenceReset</code> be sent in their place. This is accomplished by setting
the <code>MsgSeqNum</code> of the <code>SequenceReset</code> to the next expected outbound
sequence number and the <code>NewSeqNo (tag #36)</code> to the sequence number of the
highest administrative message in the group, plus one. Example: there are five
consecutive administrative messages being resent. The first message has a
sequence number of ten, the last a sequence number of fourteen. A
<code>SequenceReset</code> is sent in their place, with <code>MsgSeqNum=10</code> and
<code>NewSeqNo=15</code>.</p>
<h4 class="DocSearch-lvl4 mt-5 mb-2 text-base font-medium text-slate-700 dark:text-slate-300"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#logon-messages" id="logon-messages">Logon Messages<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h4>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">Under the standard ordered message processing and recovery paradigm,
out-of-order messages are never processed on the application layer. An exception
is made for <code>Logon</code> messages. The recipient of a <code>Logon</code> should always
process it immediately, even if their sequence number is too high. After sending
a <code>Logon</code> confirmation back, a <code>ResendRequest</code> is sent if a message gap
was detected in the Logon sequence number.</p>
<h3 class="DocSearch-lvl3 mt-7 mb-2 text-[1.0625rem] leading-snug font-semibold text-slate-900 dark:text-white"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#fix42Duplicates" id="fix42Duplicates">Duplicates<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h3>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">Messages may be duplicated in the course of typical <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="#fix42OrderedProcessing">ordered message processing</a> and <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="#fix42Recovery">recovery</a> procedures. Any message that <em>may</em> be a duplicate (transmitted in response to a <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="/documentation/fix/fix-42/#fix42ResendRequest">ResendRequest</a>) must be sent with <code>PossDupFlag=Y</code>. If <code>PossDupFlag=Y</code> is set and the message sequence number is less than the receiver’s inbound sequence number, the message is dropped. Doing so ensures once-and-only-once processing semantics for the application layer.</p>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base "><strong>If at any point a message is seen with an incoming sequence number less than
expected and the <code>PossDupFlag</code> flag is not set to <code>Y</code>, a serious error
has occurred. The only caveat to this rule which involves multiple overlapping
<code>ResendRequests</code>, is covered in the <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="#fix42SequenceResets">Sequence Resets</a> section. The recipient of a such a message should terminate the FIX session immediately via a <code>Logout</code> message. Further intervention may be required to ensure that no orders are outstanding and that all filled orders are accounted for.</strong></p>
<h3 class="DocSearch-lvl3 mt-7 mb-2 text-[1.0625rem] leading-snug font-semibold text-slate-900 dark:text-white"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#retransmissions" id="retransmissions">Retransmissions<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h3>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">The FIX protocol specifies a method for application level retransmission when no
gap was detected but there is concern that a message was not handled by the
counterparty (e.g. an order was sent but no execution reports received). This is
usually accomplished by setting <code>PossResend (tag #97)</code> to <code>Y</code>.
OneChronos does not support this aspect of FIX. Resending messages in this
fashion is unnecessary, potentially dangerous, and impossible to implement
performantly.</p>
<h3 class="DocSearch-lvl3 mt-7 mb-2 text-[1.0625rem] leading-snug font-semibold text-slate-900 dark:text-white"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#sequence-resets" id="sequence-resets">Sequence Resets<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h3>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">A counterparty’s incoming sequence number (the sender’s outgoing sequence
number) can be reset during message recovery via the <code>SequenceReset</code>
message. There are two modes of doing so:</p>
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>A <code>SequenceReset</code> message with <code>GapFillFlag (tag #123)</code> set to <code>Y</code>
(henceforth referred to <code>SeqReset-GapFill</code>)</li>
<li>A <code>SequenceReset</code> message with <code>GapFillFlag (tag #123)</code> set to <code>N</code>
(henceforth referred to <code>SeqReset-Reset</code>)</li>
</ul>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base "><code>SequenceReset</code> messages can also be sent to a counterparty, unsolicited,
when standard ordered message handling procedures need to be bypassed (this is
almost never necessary or advisable). Sending a <code>SeqReset-Reset</code> tells the
counterparty to ignore the <code>MsgSeqNum</code> field and reset its expected incoming
sequence number explicitly.</p>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">In all cases <code>NewSeqNo (tag #36)</code> should be set to the sequence number of
the message being skipped, plus one. If multiple messages are being skipped, it
should be set to the highest sequence number out of all messages being skipped,
plus one.</p>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base "><code>SeqReset-GapFill</code> messages must conform to the standard sequencing rules
(i.e. their sequence numbers should match the counterpary’s expected inbound
sequence number). Both <code>SeqReset-GapFill</code> and <code>SeqReset-Reset</code> requests
should only ever attempt to increase the counterparty’s incoming sequence
number. The note in the section on <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="#fix42Duplicates">duplicates</a> holds - any attempt to decrease a counterparty’s sequence should (and will, for OneChronos) result in immediate termination of the session.</p>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">It is possible to have multiple <code>ResendRequests</code> in-flight simultaneously.
When this happens a <code>SeqReset-GapFill</code> sent in response may be an implicit
duplicate, even if the <code>PossDupFlag</code> is not set. This condition is easily
detected by checking if the <code>MsgSeqNum</code> for a <code>SeqReset-GapFill</code> is less
than the expected incoming sequence number. Such messages should be silently
dropped, even though <code>PossDupFlag</code> is not set. As an example (adapted from
the FIX 4.2 guide):</p>
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>P1 sends a <code>ResendRequests</code> for sequence numbers five through ten followed
immediately by a second request for five through eleven.</li>
<li>Messages eight, ten, and eleven are application messages. Messages five
through seven, and nine, are administrative messages that should be skipped.</li>
<li>P2 replies to the first <code>ResendRequests</code> with
<code>(SeqReset-GapFill, MsgSeqNum=5, NewSeqNo=8)</code>,
<code>(Message eight, MsgSeqNum=8, PossDupFlag=Y)</code>,
<code>(SeqReset-GapFill, MsgSeqNum=9, NewSeqNo=10)</code>,
<code>(Message ten, MsgSeqNum=10, PossDupFlag=Y)</code>.</li>
<li>P2 replies to the second <code>ResendRequests</code> with
<code>(SeqReset-GapFill, MsgSeqNum=5, NewSeqNo=8)</code>,
<code>(Message eight, MsgSeqNum=8, PossDupFlag=Y)</code>,
<code>(SeqReset-GapFill, MsgSeqNum=9, NewSeqNo=10)</code>,
<code>(Message ten, MsgSeqNum=10, PossDupFlag=Y)</code>,
<code>(Message eleven, MsgSeqNum=11, PossDupFlag=Y)</code>.</li>
<li>P1 receives P2’s first series of replies and processes them, after which P2’s
expected incoming sequence number is eleven.</li>
<li>P1 receives P2’s second series of replies. The first of which,
<code>SeqReset-GapFill, MsgSeqNum=5, NewSeqNo=8</code>, attempts to decrease P2’s
incoming sequence number. However P2 can determine that this is an <em>implicit</em>
duplicate given that <code>MsgSeqNum=5</code> is <em>less than</em> the expected sequence, so it
ignores it. <code>Message eight, MsgSeqNum=8, PossDupFlag=Y</code> is ignored as a
duplicate because <code>PossDupFlag=Y</code> and its sequence number is less than eleven.
<code>SeqReset-GapFill, MsgSeqNum=9, NewSeqNo=10</code> is ignored for the same reason
that the first <code>SeqReset-GapFill</code> was ignored, and message ten is ignored for
the same reason that message eight was. Message eleven is processed because
<code>MsgSeqNum=11</code> is equal to the expected incoming sequence number.</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="DocSearch-lvl3 mt-7 mb-2 text-[1.0625rem] leading-snug font-semibold text-slate-900 dark:text-white"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#fixLogonProcedures" id="fixLogonProcedures">Logon Procedures<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h3>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">FIX sessions start with a <code>Logon</code> message and end with a <code>Logout</code>. Given the
complexities of sequence number handling several scenarios must be accounted
for. We will refer the party initiating the logon as Initiator and the party
receiving it as Acceptor.</p>
<h4 class="DocSearch-lvl4 mt-5 mb-2 text-base font-medium text-slate-700 dark:text-slate-300"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#logging-in-and-resetting-sequence-numbers" id="logging-in-and-resetting-sequence-numbers">Logging in and Resetting Sequence Numbers<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h4>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">A session Initiator can reset sequence numbers while logging in by including the
field <code>ResetSeqNum (tag #141)=Y</code>. In such cases, Acceptor will reset their
expected incoming sequence number to one <em>before</em> processing the <code>Logon</code>
message, per the <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="#fix42MessageDelivery">message delivery rules</a>. Messages from any previous session will be dropped. Note: this is only advisable when establishing a new FIX session after the <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="/documentation/fix/fix-42/#marketHours">daily sequence number reset period</a>.</p>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">The following logic applies when Acceptor receives a <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="/documentation/fix/fix-42/#fix42Logon">Logon</a> message
with <code>ResetSeqNum (tag #141)=Y</code>:</p>
<ol class="text-textPrimary list-inside list-decimal px-2 py-4 text-base font-normal">
<li><code>Logon</code> message received by Acceptor is valid and authentication succeeds?
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Yes
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Acceptor responds with <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="/documentation/fix/fix-42/#fix42Logon">Logon</a> <code>ResetSeqNum (tag #141)=Y</code>. Initiator should set their expected incoming sequence number to one before processing the response from Acceptor. A session is now established.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>No, the message was valid but authentication failed
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Acceptor responds with <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="/documentation/fix/fix-42/#fix42Logout">Logout</a> with the <code>Text (tag #58)</code> field set to a description of the reason for authentication failure.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>No, the message was valid but the session was in use
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Acceptor will not respond, given that doing so might corrupt sequence
numbers for the active session.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>No, the message was well formed but invalid
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Acceptor will reply with a <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="/documentation/fix/fix-42/#fix42SessionReject">Reject</a> message with <code>SeqNum=1</code>. An explanation as to why the message was invalid will be given in the <code>Text (tag #58)</code> field. When applicable <code>SessionRejectReason (tag #373)</code> and <code>RefTagID (tag #371)</code> will be populated.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>No, the message was malformed
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Acceptor will silently drop any packets containing malformed data.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>
<h4 class="DocSearch-lvl4 mt-5 mb-2 text-base font-medium text-slate-700 dark:text-slate-300"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#logging-in-without-resetting-sequence-numbers" id="logging-in-without-resetting-sequence-numbers">Logging in Without Resetting Sequence Numbers<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h4>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">The following logic is used to negotiate a FIX session when Acceptor receives a <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="/documentation/fix/fix-42/#fix42Logon">Logon</a> message with <code>ResetSeqNum (tag #141)=N</code> or unset:</p>
<ol class="text-textPrimary list-inside list-decimal px-2 py-4 text-base font-normal">
<li><code>Logon</code> message received by Acceptor is valid, authentication succeeds, and
<code>SeqNum</code> is greater than or equal to the incoming sequence number that
Acceptor expects?
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Yes
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Proceed to step 2.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>No, the message was valid but authentication failed
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Acceptor responds with <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="/documentation/fix/fix-42/#fix42Logout">Logout</a> with the <code>Text (tag #58)</code> field set to a description of the reason for authentication failure.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>No, the message was valid but the session was in use
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Acceptor will not respond, given that doing so might corrupt sequence
numbers for the active session.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>No, the message was well formed but invalid
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Acceptor will reply with a <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="/documentation/fix/fix-42/#fix42SessionReject">Reject</a> message with <code>SeqNum=1</code>.
An explanation as to why the message was invalid will be given in the
<code>Text (tag #58)</code> field. When applicable <code>SessionRejectReason (tag #373)</code>
and <code>RefTagID (tag #371)</code> will be populated.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>No, the sequence number was less than expected
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Per the note in the section on <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="#fix42Duplicates">duplicate messages</a>, Acceptor will not acknowledge the message as doing so might cause further problems.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>No, the message was malformed
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Acceptor will silently drop any packets containing malformed data.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Are sequence numbers in sync for both the Initiator and Acceptor?
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Yes
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Proceed to step 3.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>No, the <code>Logon</code> message received by Acceptor from Initiator has a sequence
number that is higher than expected
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Acceptor sends a <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="/documentation/fix/fix-42/#fix42ResendRequest">ResendRequest</a> for the missing messages. Initiator may wish to respond with <code>SeqReset-GapFill</code> if the original messages are no longer relevant. Repeat step 2.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>No, the <code>Logon</code> message received by Initiator from Acceptor has a sequence
number that is higher than expected
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>The Initiator sends Acceptor a <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="/documentation/fix/fix-42/#fix42ResendRequest">ResendRequest</a> for the missing sequence range per the <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="#fix42Recovery">standard message recovery protocol</a>. Repeat step 2.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>No, the <code>Logon</code> message received by Initiator from Acceptor has a sequence
number that is lower than expected
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Per the <a class="text-ocxPrimary hover:underline" href="#fix42Duplicates">note on duplicate messages</a>, an serious error has occurred. Initiator should not attempt to establish another session.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Has one second elapsed since a <code>Logon</code> message or a <code>ResendRequest</code> was sent
or received by either party?
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Yes
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Acceptor sends a <code>TestRequest</code> indicating that a FIX session was
established. Initiator is free to send business type messages to
Acceptor, and vice versa.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>No
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Repeat step 3.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>
<h3 class="DocSearch-lvl3 mt-7 mb-2 text-[1.0625rem] leading-snug font-semibold text-slate-900 dark:text-white"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#logout" id="logout">Logout<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h3>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">Once a FIX session is established, either party to the session can terminate it
by sending a <code>Logout</code> message. Initiator should wait for Acceptor to respond
with a reciprocal <code>Logout</code> message before closing the session. <code>Heartbeat</code>
timeout rules apply if Acceptor does not acknowledge the <code>Logout</code> message in a
timely fashion. The logout workflow is as follows:</p>
<ol class="text-textPrimary list-inside list-decimal px-2 py-4 text-base font-normal">
<li>Initiator sends Acceptor a <code>Logout</code> message.</li>
<li>Is the sequence number equal to Acceptors’s expected incoming sequence
number?
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Yes
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Acceptor responds with a <code>Logout</code> message</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>No
<ul class="text-textPrimary list-outside list-disc px-2 py-4 ps-8 text-base">
<li>Acceptor sends Initiator a <code>ResendRequest</code></li>
<li>Acceptor sends a <code>Logout</code> messages after receiving all missing messages.
Initiator can now consider the session ended.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>
<h3 class="DocSearch-lvl3 mt-7 mb-2 text-[1.0625rem] leading-snug font-semibold text-slate-900 dark:text-white"><a class="scroll-m-[120px] group hover:text-ocxPrimary flex" href="#heartbeats" id="heartbeats">Heartbeats<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" aria-hidden="true" data-slot="icon" class="ml-2 hidden w-4 group-hover:flex"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"></path></svg></a></h3>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">FIX uses a bidirectional “heartbeat” to ensure that a session is <em>logically</em>
alive, regardless of network state. When Initiator sends a <code>Logon</code> message, it
must populate the <code>HeartBtInt (tag #108)</code> field with a value stipulating the
maximum length of time (in seconds) that should elapse between messages. For
example, if P1 sets an interval of thirty seconds (expressed as an integer
number of seconds) in her <code>Logon</code> message, she expects to see a message from P2
at least every thirty seconds. If a message is not sent as part of normal
session activity, P2 must send a <code>Heartbeat</code> message instead. When P2 replies to
P1’s <code>Logon</code> message he stipulates a heartbeat interval as well.</p>
<p class="text-textPrimary py-2 text-base ">When either party to a FIX session notes that the heartbeat interval plus one
second has elapsed without a message being sent they are responsible for sending
a <code>TestRequest</code> message. If no message is received within another heartbeat
interval, the session is considered lost. A <code>Logout</code> message should be sent
(with the expectation that the counterparty might not receive it), after which
logon procedures can be reinitiated.</p> </astro-slot></article><dl class="mt-12 flex border-t border-slate-200 pt-6 dark:border-slate-800"><div><dt class="font-mono text-[0.625rem] font-medium
